publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-07-03 00:01:14 -0400.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, newspaper clippings, programs, and records.
did.abstract: Scrapbook containing letters, newspaper clippings, and programs relating to the Cornell Cosmopolitan Club; scrapbook relating to International Student Association functions at Cornell; scrapbook relating to the founding and activities of International Student Association chapters at Berlin, Bonn, Göttingen, Heidelberg, Leipzig, Munich, and Zurich (1911-1913); photographs of foreign students at Cornell and of Cosmopolitan Club activities. Also, other correspondence and records.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

defitem.label: EAD encoding:
defitem.item: Daniel J. Keeley, Oct. 2002Peter Martinez, May 2003

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, financial records, minute books, publications, memorabilia, scrapbooks and photographs relating to the Cornell University Cosmopolitan Club; correspondence includes records relating to the operation of the Club House, The International Federation of Students, Association of Cosmopolitan Clubs of America, The International Association of Ithaca, The International Student Service, Cornell University administration, correspondents include Edmund Ezra Day, Jacob Gould Shurman, John Mott and Donald C. Kerr; Financial records include financial activities and status of the Club and Association; Minute Books include official club business records of meetings and decisions; Membership records include a selection of the foudning members of the club; Publications including The Cosmopolitan Annual, Memorabilia consisting of commemorative programs and menus documenting official Club events, including Chinese Night 1906. Many of these items are autographed; Scrapbooks relating to International Student Association functions at Cornell (1932-1935); the founding and activities of International Student Association chapters at Berlin, Bonn, Gèottingen, Heidelberg, Leipzig, Munich and Zurich (1911-1913); scrapbook containg letters, newspaper clippings, photographs, christmas cards and other memorabilia documenting the Club founding, events and members; Photographs include individual and group portraits of Club members and administrative officers.

relatedmaterial.head: RELATED MATERIALS
relatedmaterial.p: See also The Cosmopolitan Student, ARP 1227, and three cups and saucer from the Cosmopolitan Club Residence (MU-562-563, 1180, 1299, 1363) in #41-7-1964.
See also Addresses and Proceedings of the Eighth International Congress of Students, LB3602.I595 1913, http://www.archive.org/details/cu31924030605277

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Cosmopolitan Club records, #37-4-441. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

controlaccess.subject: Students, Foreign.
controlaccess.corpname: Association of Cosmopolitan Clubs in the American Universities

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photo from 1913 of the house and participants of the Eighth International Congress of Students, held at Cornell by "Corda Fratres" the International Federation of Students, of which the Cosmopolitan Club was a member. A. D. White is pictured. The proceedings from this meeting were published and are available here: http://www.archive.org/details/cu31924030605277

did.unittitle: Information on a painting by Roberto Cascarini of José de San Martín

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence regarding a found painting completed in 1942, that was possibly presented to the Cosmopolitan Club and possibly owned by Liberty Hyde Bailey.
